routes.rope_note: Nontechnical talus/scree scramble via SW ridge/south face from Lake Doris camp; no rope used on standard route.
routes.corrections: No route-specific technical beta found; based on terrain description of southwest talus slopes.

routes.rope_note: Grade III-IV alpine rock in the Southern Pickets area; approach crosses glaciated terrain near Ottohorn-Himmelhorn col requiring rope for glacier travel.
routes.corrections: No dedicated route topo/gear list found; gear inferred from grade (III-IV 5.7) and typical Southern Pickets glacier-approach alpine rock norms.

routes.rope_note: Class 2, unexposed boulder/talus ridge scramble via SW ridge from Oval Lakes or W Fork Buttermilk Creek; no rope needed.
Class 2-3 scramble to red-rock summit massif above ~3100ft; comfort with exposed scrambling needed but no rope typically used.
4 pitches/500ft; descent is two 30m rappels down a gully (or Class 3 downclimb via ridge). Single 60m rope sufficient.

sling_rack.cams: 0.3-4 in single rack; small-to-medium cams protect the P4 offwidth, no #5 needed

routes.rope_note: 8 pitches/600ft, Grade III; two rappels off pre-placed sling/horn anchors for descent.

sling_rack.cams: 0.4-4 in; small cams work well on twin cracks P3-4; P8 wide crack has sparse pro

routes.rope_note: III-IV 5.10+ R — R-rating means runout, sparsely protected sections; carry extra small wires/thin gear for spaced protection.
routes.corrections: Route confirmed to exist but trip report returned 403; gear list inferred from grade/R-rating conventions, not a verified pitch-by-pitch source.

routes.rope_note: 5.9 with one A0 aid move; standard trad rack sufficient, optional aider/daisy for the A0 section.
routes.corrections: No dedicated route page/topo located; gear inferred from Wine Spires area norms and the 5.9 A0 grade.

routes.rope_note: Grade II, 4 pitches/400ft, easy 5th class rock above a hanging glacier crux (may be impassable late season). Carry ice axe/crampons for the glacier + light rock rack. Helmets required.

sling_rack.note: per MP route description: 'runners and a few pieces of gear'

routes.rope_note: Grade III, Class 4; roped snow/glacier travel with pickets near the bergschrund (~7200ft) transitioning to Class 4 rock on an exposed summit ridge.
Class 2 off-trail scramble up steep western slopes from Lake Serene; best May/June when snow covers underbrush. No technical gear.
Class 2-3 off-trail ridge/talus scramble from Borealis Pass; no rope used on standard route.
Class 3-4 scramble via Pinnacle-Plummer saddle and south gully; loose volcanic rock with exposure - helmet strongly recommended, typically climbed unroped.
4th class scramble on Pinnacle Peak; loose volcanic rock, exposure warrants helmet and caution; a short rope/handline is optional for less-experienced parties on the exposed step.
routes.corrections: No beta specifically named 'Southwest Scramble' was located; inferred from general Pinnacle Peak/Tatoosh loose-volcanic-rock terrain and the Class 4 rating.

routes.rope_note: Class 2 easy walk-up from the Pinnacle-Plummer saddle; short rock scramble near the summit, no rope needed.
Multi-pitch alpine rock, often simul-climbed; descent is a summit walk-off down sandy slopes/scree, no rappel needed.

sling_rack.note: three short (5-15ft) technical cruxes in the 5.5-5.7 range

routes.corrections: Multiple sources grade this III 5.7 overall with a 5.6 crux pitch; the provided grade of 5.6 likely reflects the crux pitch.

routes.rope_note: Class 2-3 off-trail scramble via Rainy Lake; steep, overgrown brush and mossy slab above the lake, no rope typically used.
Class 3 max via East Ridge from the Primus-Tricouni Col ('Lucky Pass'); glacier travel required to reach the col - carry ice axe, crampons, and a rope for crevasse rescue on approach.

sling_rack.cams: few pieces optional for Class 3 rock
sling_rack.note: glacier travel gear matters more than a rock rack here

routes.rope_note: Rarely climbed, described as a jagged, loose ridge (at least 4th class terrain with a 5.6 crux); standard trad rack, helmet essential for rockfall/loose rock.
routes.corrections: Only a single source mentions this route's existence; no pitch-by-pitch gear beta found.

routes.rope_note: 6 pitches, 700ft, original 1962 line up Prusik's south face; some parties finish via a 5.10a splitter variation to the summit.
4 pitches, 450ft, on Prusik's south/west face near Solid Gold; standard alpine trad rack similar to neighboring routes.
routes.corrections: Route length/grade confirmed via overlays, but no itemized gear list was found; rack inferred from comparable Prusik south-face routes.

routes.rope_note: 5 pitches, 450ft; one short (~15ft) C1 aid section near the top where the FA party pulled on gear - carry a couple aiders/daisy or be prepared to French-free. Otherwise standard trad rack.
routes.corrections: FA trip report describes climbing character and the aid section but does not itemize the rack.

routes.rope_note: 6 pitches, Grade III 5.9+; ice axe useful for early-season snow on the Aasgard Pass approach.
Class 2-3 ridge walk from Mt. Lago area; one tricky 3rd-class ledge traverse near Point 8165, otherwise easy dirt/talus, unroped.

Approach crosses the Colonial Glacier basin (crevasses, steep snow finger) - carry ice axe, crampons, and consider a rope for glacier travel; final ridge/summit is a Class 3 rock scramble, typically unroped.
